preview_text:
The model favors Turkey at 50% to win, with Paraguay at 20%. The margin reflects a meaningful but not dominant edge; the underdog retains a genuine prospect of an upset.

The 30% draw probability sits between the win chances, suggesting a competitive contest where a stalemate is plausible. The spread across outcomes—roughly 20 points from favorite to underdog—indicates neither team is heavily favored to lose, but neither is certain to advance. The match profile leans toward a genuine contest rather than a one-sided affair.

The model and sportsbook consensus align closely on the draw (30% vs 30%). Both sources rate Turkey as favorite, though the model assigns it 7 percentage points more win probability than the consensus (50% vs 43%). The model is correspondingly more confident in Paraguay's elimination. Polymarket prices show a stark divergence, treating all three outcomes as nearly symmetric—a view neither the model nor bookmakers share. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct.
